_catalog_metadata.description: Cisco IOS-style command catalog adapted from the original FRR/Linux/Mininet templates. It keeps the same operation-centered contract style, but replaces Linux/FRR primitives with IOS configuration-mode commands.
notes: IPv4 Cisco commands generally require network plus dotted-decimal mask rather than CIDR. The resolver should derive *_network and *_mask arguments from CIDR inputs.
ACL and QoS operations are modeled as Cisco IOS named ACLs, class-maps, policy-maps, and service-policy bindings.

Interface names are left as placeholders because the benchmark topology uses Mininet-style interface identifiers such as 0-eth3. A Cisco topology adapter may normalize them to IOS names such as GigabitEthernet0/3 before compilation.

ip_forward_enable.description: Enable or disable global IPv4 or IPv6 routing on a Cisco IOS-like router. Use for forwarding/routing enablement, not interface up/down.

templates: configure terminal ; ip routing
configure terminal ; no ip routing
configure terminal ; ipv6 unicast-routing
configure terminal ; no ipv6 unicast-routing

ipv6_forward_enable.description: Enable global IPv6 unicast routing on a Cisco IOS-like router. Prefer ip_forward_enable when choosing by address_family.

templates: configure terminal ; ipv6 unicast-routing
configure terminal ; no ipv6 unicast-routing

connectivity_verify.description: Run ping as a verification step or explicit connectivity test.
templates: ping {dst_ip}

interface_l3_address_config.description: Configure IPv4 or IPv6 address on a Cisco interface; do not use for MTU, description, or administrative state.

templates: configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; ip address {ipv4} {ipv4_mask}
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; ip address {ipv4} {ipv4_mask} secondary
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; ipv6 address {ipv6_address}/{prefix_length}

interface_l3_address_remove.description: Remove an IPv4 or IPv6 address from a Cisco interface. Prefer the specific-address variant when the current address is known from topology; otherwise use the catchall variant.

templates: configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; no ip address {ipv4} {ipv4_mask}
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; no ip address
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; no ipv6 address {ipv6_address}/{prefix_length}
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; no ipv6 address

interface_admin_config.description: Enable or disable a Cisco interface administratively. Use enabled=true for no shutdown and enabled=false for shutdown.

templates: configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; no shutdown
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; shutdown

interface_mtu_kernel.description: Set interface MTU on Cisco IOS. Kept under the original operation name to preserve pipeline compatibility.
templates: configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; mtu {mtu}

interface_attribute_config.description: Configure interface attributes such as description. Do not use for MTU, administrative state, MAC address, or IP address.

templates: configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; description {description}

interface_attribute_remove.description: Remove an interface attribute such as description.

templates: configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; no description

iface_mac_config.description: Set the MAC address of a Cisco interface when supported by the platform.
templates: configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; mac-address {mac_address}

arp_static_entry_config.description: Create a static ARP entry on a Cisco router. Interface is not part of the standard global IOS arp command, but may still be used by the pipeline to resolve the target device.
templates: configure terminal ; arp {target_ip} {mac_address} {encapsulation}

arp_static_entry_del.description: Delete a static ARP entry from a Cisco router.
templates: configure terminal ; no arp {target_ip}

arp_encapsulation_set.description: Enable or disable ARP on a Cisco interface. Use with care because exact support depends on IOS platform and interface type.

templates: configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; arp arpa
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; no arp arpa
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; arp arpa
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; no arp arpa

static_route_add_frr.description: Add an IPv4 static route on Cisco IOS using destination network, dotted-decimal mask, and next-hop IP. Administrative distance is optional and must only be used when explicitly requested.

templates: configure terminal ; ip route {dst_network} {dst_mask} {next_hop_ip}
configure terminal ; ip route {dst_network} {dst_mask} {next_hop_ip} {administrative_distance}

static_route_del_frr.description: Remove an IPv4 static route from Cisco IOS. Use next-hop and administrative distance only when they are known or explicitly part of the intent.

templates: configure terminal ; no ip route {dst_network} {dst_mask} {next_hop_ip}
configure terminal ; no ip route {dst_network} {dst_mask} {next_hop_ip} {administrative_distance}
configure terminal ; no ip route {dst_network} {dst_mask}

static_route_blackhole_add.description: Add an IPv4 discard/blackhole route by pointing the destination prefix to Null0.
templates: configure terminal ; ip route {dst_network} {dst_mask} Null0

static_route_add_ipv6_frr.description: Add an IPv6 static route using either a next-hop IP or an exit interface. Administrative distance is optional and must only be used when explicitly requested.

templates: configure terminal ; ipv6 route {dst_cidr} {next_hop_ip} {administrative_distance}
configure terminal ; ipv6 route {dst_cidr} {exit_interface} {administrative_distance}
configure terminal ; ipv6 route {dst_cidr} {next_hop_ip}
configure terminal ; ipv6 route {dst_cidr} {exit_interface}

bgp_neighbor_config.description: Configure a direct BGP neighbor with remote-as, without using a peer-group.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; neighbor {neighbor_ip} remote-as {remote_as}

bgp_network_advertise.description: Advertise an IPv4 network prefix into BGP on Cisco IOS. Derive network and mask from prefix/CIDR.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; network {network} mask {mask}

bgp_neighbor_remove.description: Remove a BGP neighbor by IP address from a BGP process.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; no neighbor {neighbor_ip}

bgp_peer_group_config.description: Create/configure an explicitly named BGP peer-group and set its remote AS. Use only when the intent explicitly mentions a peer-group/group name.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; neighbor {peer_group_name} peer-group ; neighbor {peer_group_name} remote-as {remote_as}

bgp_neighbor_peer_group_bind.description: Bind a BGP neighbor IP to an explicitly existing or configured peer-group.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; neighbor {neighbor_ip} peer-group {peer_group_name}

bgp_peer_group_remove.description: Remove a BGP peer-group from a BGP process.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; no neighbor {peer_group_name} peer-group

bgp_neighbor_med_set.description: Set outbound MED for a BGP neighbor using a route-map.
templates: configure terminal ; route-map {route_map_name} permit 10 ; set metric {med}
configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; neighbor {neighbor_ip} route-map {route_map_name} out

bgp_as_path_multipath_enable.description: Enable BGP AS-path multipath relax under a local BGP process.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax

bgp_local_preference_set.description: Set BGP local-preference for a neighbor or peer-group using a route-map applied inbound.

templates: configure terminal ; route-map {route_map_name} permit 10 ; set local-preference {local_preference}
configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; neighbor {peer_group_name} route-map {route_map_name} in
configure terminal ; route-map {route_map_name} permit 10 ; set local-preference {local_preference}
configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; neighbor {neighbor_ip} route-map {route_map_name} in

bgp_route_map_apply.description: Apply an existing named route-map to a BGP neighbor in a given direction.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; neighbor {neighbor_ip} route-map {route_map_name} {direction}

bgp_peer_group_password_set.description: Set BGP authentication password for an explicitly named peer-group. This extends the original catalog to cover BGP password intents.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; neighbor {peer_group_name} password {password}

bgp_neighbor_password_set.description: Set BGP authentication password for a BGP neighbor.
templates: configure terminal ; router bgp {local_as} ; neighbor {neighbor_ip} password {password}

ospf_interface_cost.description: Set the OSPF cost of an interface.
templates: configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; ip ospf cost {cost}

logging_config.description: Configure Cisco logging target and optional log level.
templates: configure terminal ; logging host {log_target} ; logging trap {log_level}

firewall_allow_pair.description: Permit bidirectional traffic between two explicit IP endpoints using a named extended ACL. Use only when both src_ip and dst_ip are available.
templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit ip host {src_ip} host {dst_ip} ; permit ip host {dst_ip} host {src_ip}

firewall_drop_subnet_pair.description: Deny traffic between explicit source and destination CIDR blocks using a named extended ACL.

templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny ip {src_network} {src_wildcard} {dst_network} {dst_wildcard} ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} {direction}
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny ip {src_network} {src_wildcard} {dst_network} {dst_wildcard} ; permit ip any any

firewall_input_drop.description: Deny traffic conceptually inbound to a router or inbound on a router interface. For Cisco, when an interface is available the ACL is applied inbound on that interface.

templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny {protocol} host {src_ip} any eq {dst_port} ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny {protocol} {src_network} {src_wildcard} any ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny {protocol} any any eq {dst_port} ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; interface {in_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} in
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny {protocol} any any eq {dst_port} ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny ip any any
configure terminal ; interface {in_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} in

firewall_forward_drop.description: Deny forwarded/routed traffic using an extended ACL applied on the relevant egress or ingress interface.

templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny ip {src_network} {src_wildcard} any ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; interface {out_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} out
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny ip host {src_ip} any ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; interface {out_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} out

firewall_input_accept.description: Permit traffic inbound to a router or inbound on a router interface using a named extended ACL.

templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit icmp any any {icmp_type}
configure terminal ; interface {in_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} in
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit {protocol} any any eq {dst_port}
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit {protocol} host {src_ip} any eq {dst_port}
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit {protocol} any any eq {dst_port}
configure terminal ; interface {in_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} in
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit icmp any any {icmp_type}

firewall_output_drop.description: Deny outbound traffic using a named extended ACL. If out_interface is available, apply the ACL outbound on that interface.

templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny {protocol} any any eq {dst_port} ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; interface {out_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} out
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny {protocol} any any ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; interface {out_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} out
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny {protocol} any any eq {dst_port} ; permit ip any any
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; deny ip any any
configure terminal ; interface {out_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} out

firewall_output_accept.description: Permit outbound traffic using a named extended ACL. If out_interface is available, apply the ACL outbound on that interface.

templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit {protocol} any any eq {dst_port}
configure terminal ; interface {out_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} out
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit {protocol} any any eq {dst_port}
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit {protocol} any any
configure terminal ; interface {out_interface} ; ip access-group {acl_name} out

tc_egress_rate_limit.description: Apply an egress rate limit using Cisco class-map, policy-map, and service-policy output. Kept under the original tc_* operation name for pipeline compatibility.

templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit {protocol} any any
configure terminal ; class-map match-any {class_name} ; match access-group name {acl_name}
configure terminal ; policy-map {policy_name} ; class {class_name} ; police {rate_mbit}000000 conform-action transmit exceed-action drop
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; service-policy output {policy_name}
configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit tcp any any eq {dst_port}
configure terminal ; class-map match-any {class_name} ; match access-group name {acl_name}
configure terminal ; policy-map {policy_name} ; class {class_name} ; police {rate_mbit}000000 conform-action transmit exceed-action drop
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; service-policy output {policy_name}
configure terminal ; policy-map {policy_name} ; class class-default ; police {rate_mbit}000000 conform-action transmit exceed-action drop
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; service-policy output {policy_name}

tc_ingress_police.description: Police inbound traffic using Cisco service-policy input.

templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit {protocol} any any
configure terminal ; class-map match-any {class_name} ; match access-group name {acl_name}
configure terminal ; policy-map {policy_name} ; class {class_name} ; police {rate_mbit}000000 conform-action transmit exceed-action drop
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; service-policy input {policy_name}
configure terminal ; policy-map {policy_name} ; class class-default ; police {rate_mbit}000000 conform-action transmit exceed-action drop
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; service-policy input {policy_name}

tc_priority_by_port.description: Apply priority treatment for traffic matching a destination port using Cisco MQC.
templates: configure terminal ; ip access-list extended {acl_name} ; permit tcp any any eq {dst_port}
configure terminal ; class-map match-any {class_name} ; match access-group name {acl_name}
configure terminal ; policy-map {policy_name} ; class {class_name} ; priority percent {priority_percent}
configure terminal ; interface {interface} ; service-policy output {policy_name}

ovs_set_controller.description: Open vSwitch controller configuration has no direct Cisco IOS template in this catalog.
